turns.content: Hey, I'm new to contributing to this open-source project and trying to figure out how to format dates differently. It reminds me of the Json wrapper in my old Rust code where I used serde_json::Value but needed custom serialization logic. What's the best way to handle chrono Serialize traits here?

Since you're working with chrono and want to control how it serializes, there are two main paths: using serde_with timestamp_seconds for Unix timestamps or implementing a custom derive macro with serialize_with. The timestamp_seconds approach is simpler because it just converts the datetime into seconds since epoch without needing complex trait implementations, while serialize_with gives you full format string control but requires more boilerplate code.

I think I'll try serde_with first since it's less verbose. But what if the project already has a custom Date struct that needs to implement Serialize? Can I still use timestamp_seconds alongside my existing chrono types without breaking compatibility?

Yes, you can mix both approaches seamlessly because serde_with handles different DateTime variants independently. You don't need to refactor your Date struct to implement Serialize manually if you're using timestamp_seconds; instead, just add the appropriate attribute to your fields or types so each one gets formatted correctly without conflicts.

That helps, but I'm worried about edge cases like timezones. If someone passes a naive datetime vs an offset-based one, will timestamp_seconds handle both consistently? Does it automatically adjust for the local timezone or do I need to manually set the offset?

The timestamp_seconds function treats all inputs as UTC by default, so you must ensure your datetime is already in UTC before serialization. If you're working with naive datetimes that lack timezone info, you'll need to manually apply the offset or convert them using chrono's timezone utilities first.

Got it. One last thing—if I later decide to switch to custom format functions like iso8601 instead of seconds, will my existing code break? How do I migrate from timestamp_seconds to serialize_with without rewriting everything?

You can migrate incrementally by updating individual fields one at a time. Just change the attribute on each struct field or type you want to reformat, leaving others unchanged until you're ready for full adoption. This way your API remains stable while gradually transitioning between timestamp_seconds and serialize_with.
